principles: Fresh final build from source scripts; do not rely on prior generated JSON.
Preserve v5.8 guardrails while allowing slide-level creative choice.
Use the deck engine slide as a meta case study, not a technical tangent.
Keep the Messi metaphor as a bookend, not wallpaper.
Structural QA, feedback regression QA, and creative critique are mandatory.

slides.creative_intent: Premium CXO opening slide that frames AI adoption as a system-survival problem.
slides.visual_anchor: logo area and strong title block

slides.creative_intent: Use the Messi metaphor once as a key-person-risk hook.
Make AI deployment failure feel like operating risk, not training friction.
Show AI as an industrialiser of the operating model.
Reframe enterprise AI as operating discipline rather than software procurement.
Show SHIFT as the executive architecture and failure-mode map.
Open proof layer with a shift from argument to evidence.
Contrast prompt stacks with governed systems.
Show VEDA as an engineered build process with five design decisions.
Make VEDA look like a governed operating architecture.
Map VEDA to SHIFT as built evidence.
Use the deck engine as a meta case study in agentic engineering.
Translate software engineering concepts into agentic engineering practice while clarifying autonomy versus automation.
Translate VEDA lessons into CXO-level rules.
Return to the Messi metaphor as payoff: raising the floor.
Make the 12-month path sequenced and CFO-legible.
Close the scale argument through control, not hype.
Close commercially while keeping the system argument alive.
Legal and usage notes without interfering with the deck story.
